intro: Koeleria veresczaginii, also known as Veresczagin's hair grass or Koeleria, is a perennial grass species belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria veresczaginii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Koeleria veresczaginii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory conditions and promote relaxation.
